notes: Lesson 19 Part 2 (act 110), 'The size-k min-heap trick', 83.8s, 6 cues. Find the k-th largest with a size-k MIN-heap (k=3, stream 5,2,8,1,9,3,7): fill first 3 -> {2,5,8} root 2; 9 arrives, push over cap, pop root 2 -> {5,8,9} root 5; after the run the 3 largest are {7,8,9} and root 7 = the 3rd largest (sorted desc 9,8,7,5,3,2,1 -> 3rd is 7). Contrast MIN vs MAX heap (min keeps the smallest winner on the chopping block; max would evict the biggest = wrong), complexity O(n log k) with k slots, misconception. Uses heap.py (heap_tree, cap_badge, recolor_node, root_marker) + arrays.py value_row; MathTex reserved for the n log k bound. Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke every cue, then full -ql render (6 chunks) + READ start/mid/end for all 6 cues. cue04_end / cue05_end are the documented keyframe-seek boundary / EOF artifacts (cue04_end lands on cue05's opening headline; cue05_end at 83.65s exceeds the 83.668s video); both settled states verified via mid frames + a manual mid-cue extract at 82.5s — all content correct. Per-cue checklist (legibility, math/algorithm correctness, highlight matches beat, contrast, safe area, chunk timing): all pass. Final -ql 83.668s vs audio 83.76s (delta 0.092s).
